author: John Kanell
description: Chicken Florentine is a comforting Italian-inspired dish featuring pan-seared chicken breasts served in a garlicky spinach cream sauce with parmesan. It's quick, easy, and made with simple everyday ingredients.
instructions: In a bowl, combine the flour, salt, and pepper and whisk together.
Pound the chicken breasts with a mallet until about 1 inch thick.
Season both sides of the chicken with salt and pepper, then dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add two chicken breasts, cover, and cook for about 4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (internal temperature of 165°F). Remove to a plate and repeat with remaining chicken using another tablespoon of butter.
Reduce heat to medium. Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter in the skillet, add garlic, and cook for 30-45 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the white wine and cook, stirring occasionally, until reduced by about half.
Stir in the cream and bring to a simmer. Add the parmesan cheese and stir until melted.
Add the spinach and cook until wilted. Return the chicken and any juices to the pan, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer until the sauce thickens and the chicken is warmed through, about 4 minutes.
Garnish with parsley if desired and serve immediately.
notes: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs can be used instead of chicken breasts.
Swiss chard or kale can be substituted for spinach.
If you don't have white wine, substitute with chicken stock.
Mushrooms can be added and sautéed alongside the garlic.
Serve immediately for best texture, as cream sauces thicken as they cool.
personalNotes: Honestly it wasn't that great. I used a enameled cast iron and I think it was too hot. Also it will be a bit of work to clean the cast iron after cooking if the pan is too hot and food/sauce on the surface burns. I would cook it again but it was just okay. The leftovers were better with rice. I may have liked it with a little more cheese. Also it took me 2 hours to prep and cook this for some reason.
title: Chicken Florentine
